tests.description: Measures throughput when every subject is in its strongest crash-safe disk-buffer mode (fsync per write / reliable / crash_resistance). Quantifies the EPS cost of zero-loss durability against the baseline disk_buffer_performance test.
Measures throughput with disk-based buffering enabled
Verifies events survive subject restart when disk buffering is enabled
Verifies no data loss when log files are rotated by creating a new file

Verifies that a file-tail subject can recover when it's offline across a rotation: subject reads partway, gets SIGTERM'd, generator rotates the log file (create mode) and writes more events to the new file while subject is offline, then subject restarts and must catch up on the un-read tail of input.log.1 + the new input.log without re-emitting anything already forwarded. A subject that only watches /data/input.log by path (no glob, no persistent state) will lose every un-read pre-rotation event in input.log.1 — that's the case this test catches.
Verifies no data loss when log files are rotated by truncation
Measures throughput of tailing a file and forwarding over TCP
Verifies no data loss when a log file is directly truncated
Measures the rate at which a NetFlow v5 listener can decode binary records and forward them as TCP lines.

End-to-end OTLP/gRPC correctness head-to-head: each subject receives OTLP/gRPC and forwards as OTLP/gRPC. Validates the full receive→forward path is zero-loss across vmetric, otel-collector, rotel, fluent-bit, grafana-alloy, bindplane-agent, cribl-stream, and axosyslog. vector is excluded — its OTLP sink is HTTP-only as of 2026.

End-to-end OTLP/gRPC throughput head-to-head: each subject receives OTLP/gRPC on 4317 and forwards as OTLP/gRPC. Isolates per-protocol overhead vs. the HTTP+protobuf generic case. vector is excluded — its OTLP sink is HTTP-only as of 2026.

OTLP/HTTP receive→forward with one pipeline-stage transform per record (add a static attribute). Validates the per-record decode → mutate → re-encode round-trip preserves the LogRecord body bytes. rotel is excluded — its built-in transform layer doesn't expose a portable add-attribute primitive.

OTLP/HTTP receive→forward with one pipeline-stage transform per record (add a static attribute). Measures the per-record decode → mutate → re-encode cost on top of the generic case. rotel is excluded — its built-in transform layer doesn't expose a portable add-attribute primitive (Python processor mount would be required, which we don't ship).

End-to-end OTLP/Logs correctness head-to-head: each subject receives OTLP/HTTP+protobuf and forwards via OTLP/HTTP+protobuf. Verifies the full receive→forward path and zero-loss across vmetric, vector, fluent-bit, otel-collector, rotel, grafana-alloy, bindplane-agent, and cribl-stream.

End-to-end OTLP/Logs throughput head-to-head: rate at which each subject can receive OTLP/HTTP+protobuf and forward it as OTLP/HTTP+protobuf. vmetric vs. rotel vs. otel-collector vs. vector vs. fluent-bit vs. grafana-alloy vs. bindplane-agent vs. cribl-stream.
Simulates a real-world pipeline with parsing, filtering, and routing
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with a regex mask applied to every record (e.g. CONN=<digits> → CONN=***).
Measures baseline pipeline engine overhead: add one field per record using each subject's native transform
Verifies no data loss during config reload via SIGHUP
Measures throughput of syslog message parsing in the pipeline
Measures baseline overhead of receiving TCP with no output (blackhole sink)
Measures sustained throughput of TCP-in to HTTP-out pipeline (5 minutes)
Measures throughput of TCP-in to HTTP-out pipeline
Verifies that logs sent while the HTTP receiver is down are persisted and delivered once the receiver starts
Measures sustained throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with no transforms (5 minutes)
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with no transforms
Verifies that logs sent while the receiver is down are persisted and delivered once the receiver starts
Verifies durable persistence across an ungraceful subject crash (SIGKILL): send logs, kill subject without grace, start receiver, restart subject, verify all logs arrive
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with disk persistence enabled
Verifies durable persistence across a subject restart: send logs, stop subject, start receiver, restart subject, verify all logs arrive
Verifies JSON-in-string fields are parsed and re-emitted correctly
